name: Yellowfin Tuna
habitat: Open ocean, tropical and subtropical waters
description: A large, fast-swimming pelagic fish prized for sport fishing and sushi. Identified by bright yellow dorsal and anal fins.
funFacts: Can swim up to 75 km/h
Highly migratory — crosses entire ocean basins
Warm-blooded, maintaining higher body temperature than surrounding water
colorPattern: metallic blue-black back, silver belly, bright yellow fins

name: Mahi Mahi
habitat: Surface waters of tropical and subtropical oceans
description: Also called dorado, recognized by its brilliant golden-green coloring and blunt forehead. Colors fade quickly after death.
funFacts: One of the fastest-growing fish — reaches full size in 1 year
Can change colors rapidly when excited or dying
Males have a distinctive square forehead (females are rounded)
colorPattern: vivid golden-green flanks, blue-green back

name: Red Snapper
description: Popular sport and food fish with distinctive red coloring and large red eyes. Often found near structures and reefs.
funFacts: Can live over 50 years
Red eyes help them see in deep, darker water
Juveniles have a dark spot on their sides that fades with age
colorPattern: uniform reddish-pink body, red eyes

name: Clownfish
habitat: Coral reefs, symbiotic with sea anemones
description: Famous for their symbiotic relationship with sea anemones. The bright orange and white coloring warns predators.
funFacts: All clownfish are born male — dominant ones can become female
Immune to the stinging cells of their host anemone
Became famous as 'Nemo' in the animated film
colorPattern: bright orange with white bands bordered by black

name: Lionfish
description: Striking and venomous reef fish with elaborate fins. An invasive species in the Atlantic causing significant ecological damage.
funFacts: Venomous spines can cause extreme pain but are rarely fatal to humans
Invasive in the Atlantic — no natural predators outside native range
Can eat prey up to half their own body size
colorPattern: red/white/black bands, elaborate feather-like pectoral fins

name: Atlantic Salmon
description: Iconic migratory fish that travels from ocean to rivers to spawn. Silver in the ocean, develops red/brown coloring in freshwater.
funFacts: Returns to the exact river where it was born to spawn
Can jump up to 3.6 meters to clear waterfalls
Most die after spawning, but Atlantic salmon can survive and spawn multiple times
colorPattern: silver-blue in sea, pink-red spots in freshwater

name: Blue Marlin
description: One of the largest billfish in the world. An apex predator of the open ocean and prized sport fishing trophy.
funFacts: Uses its bill to slash through schools of fish
Can reach speeds of over 110 km/h
Featured in Hemingway's 'The Old Man and the Sea'
colorPattern: cobalt blue back, silver-white belly, pale blue vertical stripes

name: Swordfish
habitat: Open ocean, tropical to cold temperate
description: Solitary predator with a distinctive flat, elongated bill used for slashing prey. Highly migratory and valued for its firm flesh.
funFacts: Has a special organ that heats its brain and eyes for better vision
Adults have no teeth or scales
Can dive to 2,878 meters depth
colorPattern: dark metallic purple-blue back, lighter sides, white belly

name: Great Barracuda
habitat: Tropical and subtropical coastal waters, reefs
description: Fearsome predator known for its razor-sharp teeth and torpedo-shaped body. Often lurks near reefs waiting for prey.
funFacts: Attracted to shiny objects which they mistake for prey fish
Can accelerate to 43 km/h in short bursts
Large barracuda can cause ciguatera fish poisoning when eaten
colorPattern: silver with dark spots, two dorsal fins set far apart

name: Pufferfish
description: Famous for inflating into a spiky ball when threatened. Contains tetrodotoxin, one of the most potent natural toxins.
funFacts: Contains enough toxin to kill 30 adult humans
Pufferfish is a delicacy in Japan (fugu) — chefs require special licensing to prepare it
Inflates by swallowing water (not air)
colorPattern: varies widely — spotted, striped, or plain

name: Hammerhead Shark
habitat: Coastal and offshore tropical and warm temperate waters
description: Distinctive shark species with a unique hammer-shaped head that provides enhanced sensory capabilities.
funFacts: Eyes on opposite ends of the hammer give nearly 360° vision
Can detect electrical fields from prey buried in sand
Immune to stingray venom — stingray is their favorite food
colorPattern: grey-brown back, white belly, distinctive T-shaped head

name: Wahoo
description: One of the fastest fish in the ocean, prized for sport fishing. Has an elongated body and scissor-like teeth.
funFacts: Can reach speeds of 80 km/h
Named 'wahoo' after the Hawaiian island Oahu where it was commonly caught
Has no swim bladder — must keep swimming or sink
colorPattern: iridescent blue-green back, silver sides with pale blue vertical bars

name: Grouper (Hammour)
description: Known as Hammour in the Gulf region. A highly valued food fish and master of camouflage that can change color to blend into surroundings.
funFacts: All born female — dominant fish become males
Can rapidly change color and pattern to camouflage
Ambush predator — waits motionless before striking at prey
colorPattern: brown/reddish with orange-brown spots and blotches

name: Flounder
description: A flat fish that lies on the sea floor. Both eyes migrate to one side of the body, allowing the fish to see while lying flat.
funFacts: Born with one eye on each side — one eye migrates to join the other
Master of camouflage, can match almost any background in seconds
Spends most of its life lying on the seafloor buried in sediment
colorPattern: mottled brown/grey on top side, white underneath

name: Parrotfish
description: Colorful reef fish with a beak-like mouth used to scrape algae from coral. Responsible for much of the white sand on tropical beaches.
funFacts: Produces white sand through digestion of coral skeleton — up to 90 kg per year
Can change sex and color multiple times during life
Sleeps inside a mucus cocoon to mask its scent from predators
colorPattern: vibrant blues, greens, and pinks with a parrot-like beak

name: Blue Tang
description: Vibrant blue reef fish with a distinctive palette-like shape. Known as Dory from the animated film 'Finding Nemo'.
funFacts: Has a sharp spine near the tail that can inflict wounds — hence 'surgeonfish'
Changes from yellow juvenile to vivid blue adult coloring
Famous as the character 'Dory' in Finding Nemo
colorPattern: electric blue body with black 'painter's palette' marking

name: Pacific Halibut
habitat: Cold coastal and offshore waters, sandy/muddy floors
description: Largest flatfish in the world. Diamond-shaped body and both eyes on the same side. Important commercial fishery species.
funFacts: Can live over 55 years
Females grow much larger than males
Can change color to match the ocean floor
colorPattern: dark brown on top, white underneath, diamond-shaped body

name: Cobia
description: Powerful, torpedo-shaped fish often found following sharks and manta rays. Highly prized as both a sport and food fish.
funFacts: Often follows larger marine animals to feed on their scraps
Curious fish — will sometimes follow divers closely
One of the fastest-growing fish in aquaculture
colorPattern: chocolate brown with white/silver lateral line stripe

name: Titan Triggerfish
description: Large, aggressive reef fish with powerful teeth that can bite through coral. Will defend nest areas from divers.
funFacts: Can lock its dorsal trigger spine upright — hard for predators to swallow
Aggressively defends nest site against anything, including divers
Powerful enough to bite through coral rock
colorPattern: mottled green-grey with yellow-rimmed scales around face

name: Giant Trevally
habitat: Coastal coral reefs, lagoons, open ocean
description: Apex predator of reef environments. Known to hunt seabirds from the water's surface and travel in large schools when juvenile.
funFacts: One of few fish known to hunt seabirds at the surface
Juveniles travel in large schools, adults are often solitary
Known as 'GT' among sport fishers — a prized target species
colorPattern: silver to grey body, sometimes darker or golden in breeding season

name: Seahorse
description: Unique fish that swims upright and uses its prehensile tail to anchor to seagrass. Only fish species where the male carries and gives birth to young.
funFacts: Males become pregnant and give birth to up to 2,000 young at once
No stomach — must eat almost constantly to survive
Monogamous and can 'hold hands' with a partner using their tails
colorPattern: varies — yellow, orange, brown, red, black; excellent camouflage

name: Giant Moray Eel
description: Largest moray eel species. Lives hidden in reef crevices and hunts at night. Has two sets of jaws — second set (pharyngeal) pulls prey down throat.
funFacts: Has a second set of jaws (pharyngeal jaws) that move forward to grab prey
Breathes by constantly opening and closing its mouth — not being aggressive
Often seen with companion cleanerfish that remove parasites
colorPattern: brown with irregular black spots and blotches

name: Giant Manta Ray
habitat: Tropical and subtropical open ocean, coral reefs
description: Largest ray species in the world. Filter feeder with wing-like pectoral fins. Known for spectacular leaping behavior.
funFacts: Largest brain-to-body ratio of any fish
Congregates at cleaning stations where fish remove parasites
Leaps completely out of the water, possibly for communication
colorPattern: black dorsal surface, white ventral, distinctive cephalic fins

name: Blue-Spotted Stingray
description: Beautiful and commonly seen reef ray with vivid blue spots. Typically harmless but has venomous tail spines used in defense.
funFacts: Venomous tail spine used only for defense, not attacking prey
Often rests buried under sand to ambush prey and avoid predators
Steve Irwin's tragic death was caused by a freak stingray accident
colorPattern: yellow-green with vivid blue spots, blue-edged tail

name: Napoleon Wrasse
habitat: Coral reefs, steep outer reef slopes
description: Massive and charismatic reef fish with a distinctive hump on the forehead. One of the largest fish on coral reefs.
funFacts: Born female, changes to male as it grows
Has thick, mucus-coated lips that protect it from stinging prey
Can live over 30 years and is often friendly toward divers
colorPattern: adults are blue-green with orange-red lines; large forehead hump

name: Kingfish (Spanish Mackerel)
description: Fast, streamlined pelagic predator. One of the most prized sport and food fish in the Arabian Gulf region, where it is called Kanaad.
funFacts: Can accelerate from stationary to 70 km/h almost instantly
Travels in loose schools and can be extremely aggressive when feeding
Most valuable commercial fish in the Arabian Gulf
colorPattern: silver with wavy grey-brown stripes or spots on upper body

name: Sea Bream (Shaari)
description: Common coastal fish of the Arabian Gulf known locally as Shaari. Hardy species found in mangroves and rocky shores.
funFacts: Can survive in freshwater for extended periods
Protogynous hermaphrodite — may change from female to male
Important subsistence fishery species throughout the Gulf
colorPattern: silver-grey with yellow tinge on belly, dark edge on fins

name: Common Snook
description: Prized inshore game fish with a distinctive lateral line. Master ambush predator that lurks near structures waiting for prey.
funFacts: Has a very distinctive, pronounced lateral line running the length of the body
Protandrous hermaphrodite — males become females as they grow larger
Extremely sensitive to cold — cold snaps can cause mass mortality events
colorPattern: silver with prominent dark lateral line, yellow-tinted pelvic fins

name: Atlantic Tarpon
description: Ancient species often called 'Silver King'. Spectacular jumps when hooked make it a legendary sport fish, though rarely eaten.
funFacts: One of the most ancient fish groups — unchanged for 100 million years
Can gulp air at the surface to survive low-oxygen waters
Can leap over 2.4m out of the water when hooked
colorPattern: bright silver scales, blue-green back, large eyes

name: Beluga Sturgeon
habitat: Large rivers, Caspian and Black Seas
description: Largest freshwater fish and one of the oldest fish lineages. Source of the most prized beluga caviar. Critically endangered due to overfishing.
funFacts: Can live over 100 years and weigh over a tonne
Produces the most expensive caviar in the world
Has been on Earth for over 250 million years — survived the dinosaur extinction
colorPattern: grey-green to dark grey, shark-like body with rows of bony scutes

name: Atlantic Cod
habitat: Cold North Atlantic waters, rocky bottoms
description: Historically one of the most important food fish in the world. Collapse of Grand Banks fishery in 1992 is one of the greatest ecological disasters in history.
funFacts: A single female can produce up to 9 million eggs per spawning
Has a distinctive chin barbel used for feeling along the seafloor
The word 'cape' in Cape Cod was named after the fish's abundance there
colorPattern: brown-green with spots, lighter belly, distinctive chin barbel

name: Emperor Fish (Bayadh)
habitat: Coral reefs, seagrass beds, sandy lagoons
description: Known as Bayadh or Sha'am in the Arabian Gulf. Popular food fish with excellent white flesh, commonly found in reef areas.
funFacts: Can rapidly change color when stressed or communicating
Plays an important role in controlling sea urchin populations
Highly valued in Arabian Gulf fish markets
colorPattern: silver-grey with yellow tinge, large eyes, thick lips

name: Rainbow Trout
habitat: Cold, clear freshwater rivers and lakes
diet: Insects, worms, fish eggs, small fish
description: One of the most popular sport fish in the world. Raised extensively in aquaculture and introduced to cold-water rivers on every continent except Antarctica.
funFacts: Steelhead trout are the same species — just the anadromous (sea-going) form
Pink flesh color comes from carotenoid pigments in their crustacean diet
Can see into the ultraviolet spectrum
colorPattern: silver-green with distinctive pink-red lateral stripe, black spots

name: Northern Pike
habitat: Cool freshwater lakes, rivers, weedy shallows
description: Top freshwater predator with needle-like teeth and exceptional camouflage. Solitary ambush predator hiding in aquatic vegetation.
funFacts: Has a sensory organ (the lateral line) that detects vibrations in water
Can lunge at prey with explosive acceleration from a stationary position
Known to eat ducklings, rats, and other pike
colorPattern: olive-green with cream/yellow spots and markings, white belly
